national_expectations.summary: Expectations have been transformed by the last-32 escape against Senegal. A campaign that began with manager Rudi Garcia casting Belgium as underdogs who should fear nobody, and with coverage stressing this is widely seen as the golden generation's last World Cup, now carries genuine belief that a deep run is on. Foreign coverage relayed by the Belgian press describes a 'reborn' Belgium after the comeback, with outlets noting the golden generation's obituaries had already been written. The next test is framed as formidable but winnable: the co-host United States in Seattle, where Belgium have been based for four weeks, with a quarter-final place at stake.

sources.outlet: FIFA (Garcia interview, English aggregation via IANS)

national_mood.summary: Euphoria mixed with disbelief. Belgium trailed Senegal by two goals deep into the second half of their last-32 tie and were, in the words of press coverage relayed by Sporza, 'essentially already on the plane home' before Romelu Lukaku and Youri Tielemans scored in the final minutes of normal time and Tielemans converted a 125th-minute penalty to win it 3-2 after extra time. Sporza's own reaction coverage celebrated a team that 'played with guts' and kept believing in the remontada, while the round-up of international reaction carried framings like 'to hell and back' and 'an ode to football, not for the faint-hearted'. The mood pieces explicitly link the night to happy memories of the 2018 Japan comeback. Beneath the joy sits an acknowledged edge of controversy: the winning penalty came after a roughly seven-minute VAR review and was called harsh by British pundits, a debate the Belgian press has also covered.

manager_situation.summary: Rudi Garcia, appointed in January 2025 to replace Domenico Tedesco on a deal through this World Cup, emerges from the Senegal tie both vindicated and briefly embroiled in controversy. Facing elimination at 2-0 down, he made bold 56th-minute substitutions, withdrawing Kevin De Bruyne and Jeremy Doku, and the changes were credited in coverage with sparking the comeback. Post-match he explained Belgium had banked on Senegal retreating to protect their lead, calling that approach a serious mistake; when the remarks were read as a jab at Senegal he pushed back, insisting Senegal deserved to go through as much as Belgium and that his point was general. He has consistently embraced an underdog framing and now prepares his side to face the co-host USA in front of a home American crowd.

historical_context.summary: Belgium are at their fourth straight World Cup, their best result the 2018 third place with the so-called golden generation, followed by a group-stage exit in Qatar 2022. The Senegal comeback slots straight into that history: Belgian coverage explicitly recalled the 2018 last-16 win over Japan, when Belgium also came from 2-0 down to win 3-2, almost on the same calendar date. The round-of-16 pairing with the USA is itself a rematch heavy with memory: at Brazil 2014 Belgium beat the United States 2-1 after extra time in the last 16, the night of Tim Howard's sixteen-save performance, with De Bruyne and Lukaku, both still in this squad, scoring the extra-time goals. Sporza has this year revisited that 2014 tie as the match in which 'the world discovered the Devils'.

dominant_storylines.storyline: The great escape: 2-0 down to Senegal (Habib Diarra 25', Ismaila Sarr 51') and heading out until substitute Romelu Lukaku (86') and Youri Tielemans (89') struck in three minutes to force extra time, before Tielemans converted a 125th-minute penalty for a 3-2 win. Foreign press relayed by Sporza framed it as one of the most improbable comebacks in recent tournament history, a 'resurrection' of a team whose golden-generation obituaries were already being written.

A controversial winner: the decisive penalty was awarded after a roughly seven-minute VAR review for Lamine Camara's foul on Tielemans, spotted in the build-up to Dodi Lukebakio hitting the bar. Sky Sports pundits Gary Neville and Roy Keane questioned the call, Keane calling it very harsh, and Garcia's own remark that Senegal's retreat was 'a serious mistake' triggered a post-match press-conference flashpoint before he clarified that Senegal deserved to go through as much as Belgium.

Echo of Japan 2018: the Belgian press immediately connected the night to the golden generation's most famous comeback, the 3-2 last-16 win over Japan at Russia 2018 from 2-0 down, noting the near-identical scoreline and near-identical calendar date, and casting the Senegal escape as proof the old character survives in the ageing core.

Tielemans the hero: the midfielder scored the 89th-minute equalising header, then, after the marathon VAR review, held his nerve to bury the 125th-minute penalty he had himself won, saying afterwards he was ready to take it. Lukaku's night carried its own emotional charge: the substitute scored the goal that started the comeback and afterwards suggested his father, watching from above, had helped him.

Garcia's gamble: facing elimination, the coach withdrew Kevin De Bruyne and Jeremy Doku in the 56th minute, substitutions reported as tactical and widely credited with bringing Belgium to life; post-match he explained Belgium had anticipated Senegal dropping deep to protect the lead and set out to exploit it.

Into the lion's den, but at home in Seattle: Belgium now meet co-host USA in the round of 16 in front of a home American crowd, in what is billed as Seattle's final match of the tournament and an expected sellout. The Belgian press flips the hostile-venue frame: Belgium have been based in Seattle for four weeks and this will be their third straight match at the same stadium, an edge in routine and zero travel. Sporza also flags the awkward kick-off for fans back home, deep in the Belgian night.

A rematch of the Tim Howard game: for the second time in their last three World Cups, USA meet Belgium in the round of 16. At Brazil 2014 Belgium won 2-1 after extra time despite Tim Howard's sixteen saves, with De Bruyne and Lukaku, both involved again in 2026, scoring the extra-time goals. Sporza revisited that tie this year as the match in which the world discovered the Red Devils, and American coverage leans on the revenge angle.

The USA without their spearhead: American striker Folarin Balogun, scorer of the winner against Bosnia and Herzegovina, was sent off in that match and is reported as suspended for the Belgium tie, a development Belgian coverage treats as a significant break for the Red Devils, while Sporza notes speculation about American pressure to have the suspension lifted.

The golden generation's last dance, rewritten: what began the tournament as an elegy, an ageing core at its final World Cup after two stuttering group draws, has become a resilience story, with the veterans De Bruyne, Lukaku and Courtois central to a side the international press now describes as reborn.

attributed_quotes.quote: At 2-0, we knew they would do everything to protect their goal, which in my opinion is a serious mistake.
attributed_quotes.context: Post-match, explaining how Belgium anticipated and exploited Senegal's retreat while 2-0 up; he later clarified the remark was a general tactical point, not a jab at Senegal.

attributed_quotes.quote: In extra time, it was like a boxing match, with two teams trading blows.
attributed_quotes.context: Describing the frantic extra-time period against Senegal before Tielemans' 125th-minute penalty settled it.

attributed_quotes.quote: I was ready to take the penalty.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Ik was klaar om penalty te nemen.
attributed_quotes.speaker_role: Belgium midfielder, scorer of the equaliser and the extra-time penalty winner
attributed_quotes.context: After winning and converting the 125th-minute penalty, following a roughly seven-minute VAR review, that beat Senegal 3-2.

attributed_quotes.quote_original: Bij 2-0 blijf je er nog in geloven.
attributed_quotes.context: On the dressing-room belief that fuelled the comeback from 2-0 down against Senegal.

attributed_quotes.quote: I think my father up above helped me.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Ik denk dat mijn vader hierboven me heeft geholpen.
attributed_quotes.speaker_role: Belgium striker, scored the 86th-minute goal that started the comeback
attributed_quotes.context: Emotional post-match reflection after coming off the bench to score against Senegal, invoking his father watching over him.

local_availability_reporting.status: Reported to have been struggling with an injury since the start of the tournament and was not expected to be fit for the Senegal tie; no fresh update sourced on his availability for the USA match.

gaps: No official RBFA or FIFA team-news/medical bulletin sourced after the 120-minute Senegal match; there is no verified fitness picture for the USA tie beyond the absence of reported new injuries.
De Bruyne and Doku were withdrawn in the 56th minute against Senegal; coverage frames the substitutions as tactical, not injury-related, and no injury inference is made here.

Reviewer note (allowlist): Sporza (VRT) is Flanders' public-broadcaster sport service and a primary national outlet, used under 'other'. FOX Sports (US World Cup rights holder), Sports Mole, Al Jazeera and World Soccer Talk are also not on the allowlist and are used under 'other'; the World Soccer Talk Debast item is a single weaker source and is flagged as such.

A claim that Tielemans' 125th-minute penalty is the latest winning goal in World Cup history appeared in aggregated search snippets but could not be verified on-page in a cited article, so it is omitted.
Goal-minute discrepancies between reports (Lukaku 86' vs 87', Tielemans 89' vs 90'); Sky Sports timings are used here.
Balogun's suspension for the USA is reported as the normal consequence of his red card; Sporza noted speculation about pressure to overturn it, so it is treated as reported rather than certain.

Dedicated Belgian preview coverage of the USA tie beyond Sporza's practical guide (and any Garcia pre-match press conference) was still thin as of 2026-07-02; no fresh manager or player quotes about facing the USA were sourced.
No francophone (RTBF/DH/La Derniere Heure) reaction piece was captured this cycle; the mood picture rests on Flemish (Sporza) and international coverage.
Captain identity not independently re-confirmed for the knockout phase, so no captain claim is asserted in the body.
The 2014 Belgium-USA echo is sourced to FOX Sports (US) and a pre-tournament Sporza reconstruction; a post-draw Belgian article explicitly replaying the 2014 angle for this rematch was not yet found.

Belgian dressing-room reaction; verbatim Tielemans, Courtois and Lukaku quotes; 2018 Japan comeback echo
Foreign-press round-up ('to hell and back', 'reborn', golden-generation obituaries) as relayed by the Belgian broadcaster
USA-tie preview: Seattle base-camp advantage (four weeks, third straight match at the stadium), night-time Belgian kick-off, Balogun suspension framing
Verbatim Garcia post-match quotes (serious-mistake analysis, boxing-match line) and his clarification
The Garcia post-match press-conference controversy and clarification
USA-Belgium R16 confirmation, 2014 Tim Howard game (2-1 AET, De Bruyne/Lukaku extra-time goals, 16 saves), Balogun red card/suspension
De Bruyne/Doku 56th-minute tactical substitutions while facing elimination
Cross-check of scoreline, penalty circumstances and next-round pairing (notes 87'/90' minute variance vs Sky)
Belgian-press memory of the 2014 Belgium-USA tie ('the match in which the world discovered the Devils')
Sole source for the Zeno Debast injury doubt (flagged weak in gaps)
Retained golden-generation/historical framing from the group-stage cycle

source_data_snapshot.outlet: FIFA (Garcia interview, English aggregation via IANS)
